defects: Spelling/fidelity: Header: Expected 'Halliburton Field Services — Mass Recruitment' but image shows only 'HALLIBURTON' at top and 'OIL & GAS' as a main header; 'Field Services — Mass Recruitment' is missing or not visible as header.
Spelling/fidelity: Position: Expected 'Scaffolders (3)' but image shows 'Craffolders'.
Spelling/fidelity: Position: 'Coiled Tubing (5)' vs image: Left column 'Coiled Tubing' (no number), right column 'Coiled Tubing 6 openings' (should be 5 openings per provided facts).
Spelling/fidelity: Position: 'Baroid Fluids (5)' vs image: 'Barold Fluids 5 openings' (spelling 'Barold' instead of 'Baroid').
Spelling/fidelity: Position: 'Production Solutions (6)' vs image: 'Production Solutions 5 openings' (should be 6 openings, not 5).
Spelling/fidelity: Position: 'Heavy Drivers (6)' vs image: 'Heavy Drivers' (no number; right column positions have numbers, left does not).
Spelling/fidelity: Position: 'Warehouse Keepers (5)' vs image: 'Warehouse Keepers 5 openings' but not listed in the left column.
Spelling/fidelity: Position: 'Riggers Level 3 (2)' vs image: Listed twice, left column 'Riggers Level 3', right column 'Riggers Level 3 2 openings'.
Spelling/fidelity: Position: 'Crane Operators (4)' vs image: Only in right column as 'Crane Operators 4 openings', not in left column.
Spelling/fidelity: Benefit: Image shows 'BENEFITS: Company transport, Medical insurance' but in low contrast and hard to read.
Missing fact: Header: 'Field Services — Mass Recruitment' not present as a visible header.
Missing fact: Employer: 'Halliburton' as employer line missing explicitly—it appears as a logo/header, not as an explicit employer line.
Missing fact: Country: 'Saudi Arabia' is visible but as part of 'SAUDI ARABIA • OIL & GAS'.
Legibility: The benefits section ('Company transport, Medical insurance') is present in a faint, low-contrast font over a light background, making it hard to read.
Legibility: The interview information ('Online screening at Shortlisted candidates') is also in very low contrast and hard to read.
Visual QA (catastrophic): No clear candidate-facing hook as the dominant headline (agency name is largest on canvas)
Visual QA (catastrophic): Key opportunity (project + country/sector) does not pass the three-second visual clarity test (project, role, location) — forced careful reading
Visual QA (catastrophic): Contact CTA is not prominent enough; easy to overlook, not competitive for social/mobile
Visual QA score 67/100 is below the 85 publishable bar
Visual QA: Agency name is the dominant headline, not a candidate-facing hook about the opportunity
Visual QA: Key recruitment details (about the project, country, roles) are less visually prominent than the agency/corporate brand
Visual QA: Contact CTA is not prominent or visually dominant; phone and email are at the bottom in small size
Visual QA: Verification/trust/agency identity info crowds the bottom band, but is not clearly separated in a footer or distinct trust block
Visual QA: QR code is present but looks like an afterthought sticker, not visually integrated or designed-in

defects: Agency name is the dominant headline, not a candidate-facing hook about the opportunity

requiredCorrections.note: Replace agency name as dominant headline with a strong candidate-facing hook that clearly states the opportunity and country (e.g., 'HALLIBURTON — SAUDI PROJECT, OIL & GAS POSITIONS').
Increase the size/prominence of the contact/CTA area. Use color, size, or icon treatment so phone and email immediately stand out, especially for mobile viewing.
Reduce dead zone in image and rebalance visual hierarchy so job list doesn't feel compressed at the canvas bottom. Text/image integration needed for dynamic layout.
Increase distinction between text overlays and background image to ensure perfect readability — scrims or stronger overlays may be needed for role titles, CTA, QR.
Make QR code visually integrated — currently it looks pasted or sticker-like, which reduces the design professionalism.
